page: Google Cloud Platform m4-megamem-224 is a memory-optimized virtual machine instance belonging to the m4 server family. It features an x86_64 CPU architecture with 224 dedicated vCPUs and 2976.0 GB of system memory. This configuration yields a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of approximately 13.29 GB of RAM per core, making it suitable for applications requiring massive memory footprints.

The instance does not include local storage, meaning all storage requirements must be met via network-attached block storage. It contains zero GPUs and does not provide complimentary public IPv4 addresses. These specifications represent a tradeoff, focusing resources entirely on memory capacity and compute density rather than local storage performance or graphics acceleration.

Given the massive memory footprint and high vCPU count, this instance is designed for large-scale enterprise workloads. It is qualitatively positioned as a high-resource density instance where the cost is aligned with the extreme memory allocation. It is suited for large in-memory databases, enterprise resource planning systems, and heavy data analytics workloads that fit entirely within system memory.
description: Google Cloud Platform m4-megamem-224 is a memory-optimized instance from the m4 family, designed for memory-intensive enterprise workloads. It features 224 dedicated vCPUs on an x86_64 architecture and 2976.0 GB of RAM, providing a high memory-to-core ratio of 13.29 GB per vCPU. The instance does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, requiring external network storage for data persistence. This hardware profile is engineered for large-scale in-memory databases, real-time analytics, and enterprise resource planning systems. Its qualitative cost profile reflects its high resource density, making it a specialized choice for workloads where memory capacity is the primary bottleneck.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform m4-megamem-224 is a memory-optimized instance featuring 224 dedicated x86_64 vCPUs and 2976 GB of RAM, designed for large-scale in-memory databases and enterprise workloads.

tagline: A high-capacity memory-optimized instance featuring 224 dedicated vCPUs and nearly three terabytes of RAM for demanding enterprise workloads.

No local storage or GPU accelerators included
Designed for large-scale in-memory databases and ERP systems
